author: Ambrose of Milan
book: On the Duties of the Clergy.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Dogmatic Treatises, Ethical Works, and Sermons.
On the Duties of the Clergy.
Chapter XXV. A reason is given why this book did not open with a discussion of the above-mentioned virtues. It is also concisely pointed out that the same virtues existed in the ancient fathers.

ccel.title: NPNF2-10. Ambrose: Selected Works and Letters

source.title: NPNF2-10. Ambrose: Selected Works and Letters

title: Chapter XXV. A reason is given why this book did not open with a discussion of the above-mentioned virtues. It is also concisely pointed out that the same virtues existed in the ancient fathers.

source.title: NPNF2-10. Ambrose: Selected Works and Letters

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: Perhaps, as the different classes of duties are derived from these four virtues, some one may say that they ought to have been described first of all.

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: But it would have been artificial to have given a definition of duty at the outset, and then to have gone on to divide it up into various classes.

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: We have avoided what is artificial, and have put forward the examples of the fathers of old.

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: These certainly offer us no uncertainty as regards our understanding them, and give us no room for subtlety in our discussion of them.

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: Let the life of the fathers, then, be for us a mirror of virtue, not a mere collection of shrewd and clever acts.

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: Let us show reverence in following them, not mere cleverness in discussing them.

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: Prudence held the first place in holy Abraham.

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: For of him the Scriptures say: “Abraham believed God, and that was counted to him for righteousness;” for no one is prudent who knows not God.

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: Again: “The fool hath said, There is no God;” for a wise man would not say so.

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: How is he wise who looks not for his Maker, but says to a stone: “Thou art my father”?

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: Who says to the devil as the Manichæan does: “Thou art the author of my being”?

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: How is Arius wise, who prefers an imperfect and inferior creator to one who is a true and perfect one?

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: How can Marcion or Eunomius be wise, who prefer to have an evil rather than a good God?

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: And how can he be wise who does not fear his God?

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: For: “The fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom.”

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: Elsewhere, too, it stands: “The wise turn not aside from the mouth of the Lord, but come near Him in their confession of His greatness.”

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: So when the Scripture says: “It was counted to him for righteousness,” that brought to him the grace of another virtue.

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: The chief amongst ourselves have stated that prudence lies in the knowledge of the truth.

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: But who of them all excelled Abraham, David, or Solomon in this?

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: Then they go on to say that justice has regard to the whole community of the human race.

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: So David said: “He hath dispersed abroad and given to the poor, His righteousness remaineth for ever.”

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: The just man has pity, the just man lends.

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: The whole world of riches lies at the feet of the wise and the just.

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: The just man regards what belongs to all as his own, and his own as common property.

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: The man just accuses himself rather than others.

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: For he is just who does not spare himself, and who does not suffer his secret actions to be concealed.

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: See now how just Abraham was!

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: In his old age he begat a son according to promise, and when the Lord demanded him for sacrifice he did not think he ought to refuse him, although he was his only son.

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: Note here all these four virtues in one act.

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: It was wise to believe God, and not to put love for his son before the commands of his Creator.

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: It was just to give back what had been received.

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: It was brave to restrain natural feelings by reason.

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: The father led the victim; the son asked where it was: the father’s feelings were hardly tried, but were not overcome.

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: The son said again: “My father,” and thus pierced his father’s heart, though without weakening his devotion to God.

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: The fourth virtue, temperance, too, was there.

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: Being just he preserved due measure in his piety, and order in all he had to carry out.

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: And so in bringing what was needed for the sacrifice, in lighting the fire, in binding his son, in drawing the knife, in performing the sacrifice in due order; thus he merited as his reward that he might keep his son.

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: Is there greater wisdom than holy Jacob’s, who saw God face to face and won a blessing?

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: Can there be higher justice than his in dividing with his brother what he had acquired, and offering it as a gift?

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: What greater fortitude than his in striving with God?

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: What moderation so true as his, who acted with such moderation as regards time and place, as to prefer to hide his daughter’s shame rather than to avenge himself?

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: For being set in the midst of foes, he thought it better to gain their affections than to concentrate their hate on himself.

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: How wise also was Noah, who built the whole of the ark!

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: How just again!

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: For he alone, preserved of all to be the father of the human race, was made a survivor of past generations, and the author of one to come; he was born, too, rather for the world and the universe than for himself.

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: How brave he was to overcome the flood! how temperate to endure it!

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: When he had entered the ark, with what moderation he passed the time!

verses.book: On the Duties of the Clergy.
verses.text: When he sent forth the raven and the dove, when he received them on their return, when he took the opportunity of leaving the ark, with what moderation did he make use of these occasions!
